reasoning: The document shows strong content preservation but has four major programmatic defects, including retained headers/footers, a split list across a page break, incorrect list numbering (roman numerals converted to alphabetical), and truncated image alt text. Under the count-based routing rules, 1-5 major defects require HUMAN_REVIEW. The LLM fidelity also notes a minor loss of visual emphasis.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 5; Page 3 of 5
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p4→p5: list item ends “…ds in wells around the Lee facility. One”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 10, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... the letters 'NC' with a green 'N' and dark blue '"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document uses underline and bold styling for the key terms at the beginning of each bullet point on pages 3, 4, and 5 (e.g., 'Agriculture', 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse'). The extraction JSON loses this emphasis completely, rendering them as plain text without any markdown or formatting tags.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Agriculture– (bolded and underlined in the PDF)
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Agriculture– One citizen commented about how poultry..."
concerns.location: Page 3, unordered list item 1 (and all subsequent list items on Pages 3, 4, and 5)

action_items: Remove retained header and footer text from all pages.
Recombine the split list items spanning pages 4 and 5.
Fix the ordered list type from alphabetical to roman numerals.
Complete the truncated alt text description for the image on page 1.
Restore bold and underline formatting to the key terms in the bulleted lists on pages 3-5.
